close_reason: Already resolved: SigningBackendRegistration was already removed with explanatory comment
description: lib.rs:542-548 unconditionally registers WolfHsmBackend as a SigningBackendRegistration. lib.rs:497-508 makes every SigningBackend method return SecretError::Unavailable with the same message. The umbrella secretx::from_uri for a 'signing' role will succeed at construction (registration present) and the failure surfaces only at sign()/public_key_der()/algorithm() call time. A caller using secretx to choose between configured signing backends cannot tell that wolfhsm cannot actually sign without trying. Fix options: (a) gate the SigningBackend impl behind a future cargo feature that gets enabled when wolfhsm exposes EccP256Key::load_from_nvm; (b) remove the inventory::submit! for SigningBackendRegistration entirely until signing works; (c) add a capability-probe API on registrations so the dispatcher can filter unavailable backends. (a) or (b) is least surprising for users today.
title: SigningBackend registered with inventory but always returns Unavailable; misleads dispatcher
